sourceText: My scientific labors are inseparable from my faith, and they are also the purpose of my life.
Mendel believed that scientific inquiry and religious faith were not in conflict but rather complemented each other, giving meaning to his life.
Nature operates according to one law, and we can discover that law.
This expresses the belief that all phenomena in the world have an order and rules, and human reason can uncover those laws.
Patience and persistence are the only path to true knowledge.
This emphasizes that acquiring deep knowledge in any field requires consistent investment of time and effort.
As a wealthy tax collector during the French Revolution, Lavoisier was targeted by the revolutionary government and unjustly executed.
Despite his tragic end, his scientific contributions were widely recognized posthumously, laying the foundation for modern chemistry.
Everything must be submitted to calculation.
Emphasizes that all phenomena must be clearly demonstrated through measurement and experimentation.
Man can learn nothing until he begins to understand.
Knowledge begins with understanding, highlighting the importance of continuous learning.
The greatest fears often stem from the smallest things.
